grammar.question_text: She finished in less time __________ I did.
Which mountain is the __________ in Africa?
You shouldn’t leave your laptop on your desk. Someone __________ steal it.
The best way of avoiding malaria is to avoid being __________ by a mosquito.
I think I have __________ the terms of the contract.
When we arrived at the hotel, we discovered that the manager __________ the wrong room for us.
I don’t know this word. What __________?
There was a robbery at the bank yesterday. Do you know how much money __________?
It’s a very modern city. There aren’t __________ old buildings.
I didn’t see you. If I had seen you, I __________ hello.
I ___________ last night because I hadn’t finished my homework.
The tennis match had just _______ when it started to rain.
This shirt is too _______ for you. You need a larger size.
You always wear the same jacket. How long _______ it for?
If we _______ you what happened, you _______ us.
I _______ tennis every Thursday evening.
If you give me the camera, I _______ your picture.
Please keep this secret. You _______ tell anyone!
I came in quietly last night as everyone _______.
You’ve just had lunch. You _______ be hungry!
I’m still considering several options. I __________ my mind yet.
They __________ be home by now. They left over an hour ago and it’s not that far.
Make sure you have it done __________ Monday. The deadline isn’t flexible.
They __________ working very long hours in this job.
There are very __________ pens left. We should order some more.
We’re going for a drink. __________ with us?
How many pages __________ when he fell asleep?
She’s __________ in one of the major aeronautical companies.
Let’s take the lift. The office is __________ the fourth floor.
This house is enormous. It’s much __________ your old place.

vocabulary.question_text: This exit should only be used in case of an __________.
Who’s going to __________ the children while you’re away?
They finished their meal and asked the server for the __________.
IHis __________ for a new high-speed railway was rejected.
After a long delay, the flight __________ left six hours late.
The government must __________ the problem of high unemployment.
We employ an expert to __________ on new technologies.
The __________ for submitting university applications is this Friday.
Sorry I’m late, but I was __________ by the traffic.
Public education was one of the biggest __________ in the party’s election campaign.
I need 50 cents for a coffee. Do you have _______ for a ten-dollar bill?
Today, many people would rather _______ a film at home than go to the cinema.
All of the employees went on _______ in protest against the management’s decisions.
After some tough bargaining, we finally got a good _______ on our new house.
We can’t _______ like this any longer. Things have got to change.
The _______ of rainforests is contributing to global warming.
Vegetarian lasagna is one of my favourite _______.
Hotel reservations can be made online or through a _______.
You should call your internet service _______ about your connection problem.
Andrew _______ in Scotland and still speaks with a Scottish accent.
The discovery is considered to be the biggest scientific _________ of the decade.
Passengers with nothing to declare move quickly through __________.
I need to move some furniture. Will you __________ me your van for the weekend?
He didn’t like his new job and __________ after six months.
We wanted to keep travelling, but we __________ of money.
He was __________ from school for plagiarism and cheating.
The plane will be __________ shortly. Please make sure all tablets and mobile phones are switched off.
Some of us feared he would never __________ from his injury.
he increase in __________ gases in the Earth’s atmosphere is contributing to global warming.
Do you think the boss will mind if I take the afternoon __________?

reading_comprehension.question_text: What best describes the tone of the text above?
reading_comprehension.text_context: Sam McKnight is one of the most famous hairdressers in the world. His clients have included Princess Diana, Kate Moss and Madonna. So when he tells you to wash your hair in bottled water, you wash your hair in bottled water.

reading_comprehension.question_text: The author of this believes readers _________________ picking and eating their own mushrooms.
reading_comprehension.text_context: Here is a quick look at what British wild mushrooms are like - and which are to be avoided. If you’re planning to pick wild mushrooms, you must invest in a field guide and ideally get some personal tuition. These descriptions are intended for the casual spotter. Colours in particular may vary.

reading_comprehension.question_text: Which of the following is closest in meaning to “several different pathways” used in the text above?
reading_comprehension.text_context: Humans give off a fragrant cocktail of body odour, heat and carbon dioxide, which varies from person to person and mosquitoes use to locate their next meal. While most animals have a specific set of neurons that detect each type of odour, mosquitoes can pick up on smells via several different pathways, suggests the study, which is published in the science journal Cell.

reading_comprehension.question_text: What best describes the tone of the text above?
reading_comprehension.text_context: A group of men dressed in 19th-_______ costume enter the village square. They have puffy white shirtsleeves, embroidered vests and leather britches, they have brightly coloured flowers bedecking their hats. They are here to chase away winter, but they are not alone.

reading_comprehension.question_text: Which of the following is NOT cited as a sign of cyberbullying?
reading_comprehension.text_context: Spot it: Cyberbullying can cause kids to avoid using their phone or computer, or appear stressed when receiving an e-mail or text. They may act reluctant to attend social or school events or avoid conversations about school or friends. In extreme cases, the child will have declining grades, stop eating or have difficulty sleeping.

reading_comprehension.question_text: Which of the following words best describes the tone of the text above?
reading_comprehension.text_context: Whistling used to be commonplace, a useful thing to do while walking or washing the car. It was a pastime particularly favoured by dads. But nobody seems to do it these days. “The world tends to be a lot more hustle-bustle,” says whistling virtuoso Robert Stemmons, who specialises in two-note harmony, warbling, trilling and an array of realistic bird calls. He also offers whistling lessons over the phone. “Whistling is one of the simple things being left by the wayside,” he says.

reading_comprehension.question_text: Which of the following most closely matches the meaning of “looming” as used in the text?
reading_comprehension.text_context: Politics is the name of the game and, with a federal election looming and many state elections around the country to follow, it is here that we must strike. It is essential that First Nations people have a seat at the table and that we stand together to protect our country, our home. In some cases, we’ll need to just build our own tables.

reading_comprehension.question_text: Which of the following is the author’s purpose in the paragraph above.
reading_comprehension.text_context: The phrase “they come in all shapes and sizes” could have been coined just for the Coleoptera — the beetles. At a fragile 0.325mm long, the Colombian featherwing (Scydosella musawasensis) has a good claim to being one of the smallest free-living insects in the world, and is tinier than many single-celled creatures. At the other end of the spectrum, the obviously named titan beetle, a huge Brazilian longhorn that can snap a pencil in its jaws, reaches 167mm – about 500 times the size.

options.a: to impress readers with his knowledge
options.b: to emphasize the variety of beetles in the world
options.d: to educate readers about an old saying

reading_comprehension.question_text: Which of the following best describes the tone of the article above?
reading_comprehension.text_context: One of the most bandied-about statistics regarding flavour perception is that it is 80% governed by smell. This often surprises people when they first hear it from a local know-it-all, as it really feels as if flavour is coming from our mouths, where the food or drink is. All becomes clear, however, when you try eating while holding your nose. Try it. I just ate a strawberry with my nostrils pinched and it was like water, with none of that blush sweetness. In fact, it’s common when people first lose their ability to smell for them to think they have lost the use of their taste buds.

reading_comprehension.question_text: Select the appropriate punctuation from the list below that should be inserted in the text above.
reading_comprehension.text_context: Italy is to apply for Unesco status for espresso coffee, claiming it is “much more than a simple drink”. It follows the art of the Neapolitan pizza-maker being added to the UN agency’s list of the world’s intangible heritage in 2017 as Italy aims to secure the worldwide status for another of its successful symbols. “It is an authentic ritual ___ and an expression of our sociality that distinguishes us around the world,” said Gian Marco Centinaio, the agriculture undersecretary, confirming that the application had been submitted.

reading_comprehension.question_text: The text above says, “her heart stopped.” Which of the sentences below has the closest meaning?
reading_comprehension.text_context: When Denise Edwards saw a text on her 11-year-old son’s phone that said, “Im gonna kill you 2mrw” from an unrecognized number, her heart stopped.

reading_comprehension.question_text: Which of the following is likely to be the tone of the article which contains the above sentence?.
reading_comprehension.text_context: From the animals that become dog and cat food and the puppy farms churning out increasingly unhealthy purebred canines, to the goldfish sold by the bag and the crickets by the box, pet ownership is problematic because it denies animals the right of self-determination.

reading_comprehension.question_text: What is the best equivalent of “invasive” in the text above?
reading_comprehension.text_context: Asian giant hornets are an invasive species also known as murder hornets because they prey on smaller insects. Their nests are difficult to locate, as they tend to be in forested areas. In the US, they pose a particular threat to native hornet species and to honeybees.

options.d: done or acting according to a fixed plan or system

reading_comprehension.question_text: The author’s purpose in the text above is which of the following?
reading_comprehension.text_context: When a dog growls at you, is it angry? When a squirrel flees up a tree at your approach, is it fearful? When an elephant stands for days on a spot where another has died, is it grieving? If you live with an animal (the non-human kind) you might think the answer is obvious, but the scientific question remains tantalisingly open.

options.a: to answer common questions the reader may have
options.b: to entice the reader to continue reading
options.d: to educate the reader about a niche topic

reading_comprehension.question_text: Select the best option to fill in the gap of the text above:
reading_comprehension.text_context: The cassette is once again desirable. New and established bands have taken to releasing music on tape. From a base of basically zero, sales are way up: 129,000 pre-recorded tapes were sold in the US last year. To millennials, it ______, the cassette represents something between music and artefact.

reading_comprehension.question_text: Which of the follow most closely matches the meaning of “table” as used in the text?
reading_comprehension.text_context: “I will continue to work with communities to elevate their voices and table their ideas for acting on a climate crisis which is very much already here.”

options.a: a piece of furniture with a flat top
options.b: put off for an undetermined period of time

reading_comprehension.question_text: Which of the following is the purpose of the text above?
reading_comprehension.text_context: In the time it takes you to read this sentence, the global building industry will have poured more than 19,000 bathtubs of concrete. By the time you are halfway through this article, the volume would fill the Albert Hall and spill out into Hyde Park. In a day it would be almost the size of China’s Three Gorges Dam. In a single year, there is enough to patio over every hill, dale, nook and cranny in England.

options.c: to soften the impact of bad news

reading_comprehension.question_text: Which of the following best describes the tone of the text above?
reading_comprehension.text_context: On the margins of a 13th-century manuscript written by Holy Roman Emperor Frederick II, are more than 900 illustrations of birds and falconers. Among the usual hawks, herons, and sparrows familiar to European readers are four sketches of a bird from further afield – an Australasian cockatoo. The images are believed to be the oldest European depiction of the bird, trumping a similar discovery in a 15th-century artwork, and have sparked a reassessment of trading routes that existed more than 700 years ago.

reading_comprehension.question_text: Which of the follow most closely matches the meaning of “table” as used in the text?
reading_comprehension.text_context: Women in medieval Japan were discouraged from studying kanji – characters modelled on written Chinese which represent individual words – and began using kana, which transcribe words phonetically. A standardisation programme at the beginning of the 20th century saw 90% of the 550 characters used in kana die out. But these forgotten characters are now being kept alive by the artist and master of Japanese calligraphy Kaoru Akagawa, who became fascinated with them after deciphering letters from her grandmother.

options.a: a piece of furniture with a flat top
options.b: put off for an undetermined period of time

reading_comprehension.question_text: Which of the following words best completes the blank in the text above?
reading_comprehension.text_context: Students getting a master’s in medieval studies can take the usual kind of medieval graduate courses, for example medieval magic, medieval papacy or early medieval identities. They can also, __________, get much more intensive technical training in reading and using medieval manuscripts, and in the scholarly languages a professional medievalist needs.

reading_comprehension.question_text: he expression “red flag” in the text has a meaning closest to which of the following?
reading_comprehension.text_context: Tim Woda, a digital safety expert, explains: “Cyberbullies are often not the biggest kids on the playground or the meanest girls at school. To protect children against cyberbullying, parents need to be aware of what their kids are doing online and be willing to act quickly when they see a red flag.”

options.b: a rectangular piece of red fabric

reading_comprehension.question_text: What best describes the tone of the text above?
reading_comprehension.text_context: At this point, a glossary might be handy. “Fungi” is the overarching name for a kingdom of spore-producing organisms that feed on organic matter. “Mushrooms” are the fruiting bodies of fungi, the place where spores are produced. Mushrooms are sometimes compared to icebergs, because most of the activity is actually taking place under the surface, out of sight.

reading_comprehension.question_text: What is the best equivalent of “invasive” in the text above?
reading_comprehension.text_context: Doctors and nurses often “weight-shame” people who are overweight or obese, leaving them feeling anxious, depressed and wrongly blaming themselves for their condition, research has found. Such behaviour, although usually the result of “unconscious weight bias”, leads to people not attending medical appointments, feeling humiliated and being more likely to put on weight. The problem is so widespread around the world that health professionals need to be taught as students that excess weight is almost guaranteed in modern society and not the fault of individuals, so they treat people more sensitively.

options.d: done or acting according to a fixed plan or system

reading_comprehension.question_text: Select the word below that best fills the blank in the text above.
reading_comprehension.text_context: Until about a month ago, I _________ I knew a fair bit about coffee. I patronised good coffee bars and knew my espresso from my macchiato; I drank decent coffee at home, too, albeit (I blush) from a Nespresso machine. But my appreciation of the stuff was all about the intensity and caffeine hit, and I now look back (with more blushes) at being offered filter coffee in a bar and thinking, “Why on Earth would I want to drink that?”

reading_comprehension.question_text: Which of the following best describes the tone of the text above?
reading_comprehension.text_context: Plants make food to grow via photosynthesis (which means “putting together with light”) converting carbon dioxide and water into sugars using the energy in sunlight. This energy is captured by chlorophyll, the green pigment found in leaves and green parts of plants. The sugars produced by photosynthesis are transported around the tree and stored in the leaves.

reading_comprehension.question_text: Which of the following is closest in meaning to “ramp up” as used in the above paragraph?
reading_comprehension.text_context: About 87,000 young children are diagnosed with lead poisoning in the US each year, more than three decades after the neurotoxin was banned as an ingredient in paint, gasoline and water pipes. Today, lead lingers in houses and apartments, yards and water lines, and wherever states and communities ramp up testing, it becomes clear that the nation’s lead problem is worse than we realized, experts say.

reading_comprehension.question_text: Which of the following is NOT a reason cited in the text for the recent mortality rate on Everest?
reading_comprehension.text_context: All climbers seeking a permit for Everest must have prior high altitude mountaineering experience and demonstrable training, a high-level commission for the Nepalese government has ruled. The recommendation was issued by the body charged with looking at the issue of high-altitude safety after one of the deadliest seasons in recent years on Everest, which was blamed on inexperience and crowding near the summit.

options.a: large groups near the top of the mountain
options.c: missing permits for climbing the mountain

reading_comprehension.question_text: Which of the following most closely matches the definition of grim as used in the text above?
reading_comprehension.text_context: An investigation has found that hundreds of thousands of tons of US plastic are being shipped every year to poorly regulated developing countries around the globe for the dirty, labour-intensive process of recycling. The consequences for public health and the environment are grim.

reading_comprehension.question_text: Which two words best complete the blanks in the text above?
reading_comprehension.text_context: An enormous ____________ for many US teenagers from disadvantaged backgrounds is when they ________ their high school diploma. High school graduates are less likely to commit crimes, have access to better paying jobs, and even have lower rates of heart disease!

reading_comprehension.question_text: Which of the following CANNOT be used to fill in the blank space above.
reading_comprehension.text_context: Level seven of a multi-storey car park in southeast London isn’t where you’d expect to hear Sibelius’s Fifth Symphony. ___________, that’s where the Multi-Story orchestra are performing.
